v1alpha1.description: KusoService is one deployable process inside a KusoProject.

command.description: argv override; only consulted for runtime=worker.

displayName.description:
Free-form label shown in the UI (canvas chip,
overlay header). Decoupled from the CR name + URL
slug so renaming the visual label is fast and
doesn't recreate kube resources. Validation:
letters / digits / spaces / hyphens, max 60 chars.
Empty = UI falls back to the slug.

fromService.description:
For runtime=worker: name of the sibling KusoService
whose image this worker should use. Empty = build from
the worker's own repo.

github.description:
Per-service GitHub App installation override. Lets
different services in one project bind to different
installations (different orgs, accounts). Same wire
shape as KusoProject.spec.github — installationId
is what the build pod uses to mint clone tokens.

image.description:
Registry-image pointer for runtime=image services
(no kaniko build). The propagation step copies
this onto every KusoEnvironment so the chart
pulls the tag directly. Other runtimes leave it
nil; the build poller writes the env's Image
after a successful build.

internal.description: When true, skip the Ingress entirely. The in-cluster Service still exists so sibling pods can reach the service via ${{ svc.URL }}, but no public domain or TLS is provisioned. Mirrored onto every KusoEnvironment owned by this service.

placement.description: AND-of-labels node selector + explicit node allowlist. Empty/missing means "schedule anywhere". The env helm chart fans this out to nodeSelector + topologySpreadConstraints.

previews.description:
Per-service preview opt-out. When disabled=true the
service is excluded from PR previews even when the
project-level toggle is on. Useful for shared
workers, crons, internal-only services that don't
make sense as throwaway URLs. Nil = inherit the
project setting.

privateEgress.description: When true, this service's pods are denied public internet egress (sibling pods, DNS and the in-cluster registry stay reachable). Default false — pods can reach the internet. Mirrored onto every KusoEnvironment owned by this service.

release.description:
Pre-deploy release hook. When set, the build poller
runs `command` as a Job using the NEW build's image
+ this env's effective envVars/envFromSecrets BEFORE
promoting the image onto the deployment. Heroku-
style — typical use is `./bin/migrate` or
`npx prisma migrate deploy`.

On non-zero exit (or timeout) the build is marked
release-failed and the image tag is NOT patched —
existing pods keep running on the previous image.
A notify event fires so a failed migration doesn't
silently bury itself in the build history.

The Job is named `<env>-release-<short-image-tag>`,
so re-deploying the same tag is a no-op (Job exists,
already succeeded). Mirrored onto every
KusoEnvironment owned by this service.

command.description: argv for the release Job container. Empty list = no release hook (equivalent to omitting the release block).

timeoutSeconds.description: Hard cap on the Job. On timeout the build is marked release-failed. Default 900s (15min).

runtime.description:
Build/runtime mode. "worker" is special — same image as
a sibling web service, but no ingress and a custom
command. Pair it with spec.command and spec.fromService
pointing at the web sibling whose image to reuse.

sharedEnvKeys.description: Per-service opt-in list of keys to inherit from project-shared (<project>-shared) and instance- shared (kuso-instance-shared) Secrets. Operator chart mounts only these keys via individual env entries (no blanket envFrom), so adding a new key to a shared secret does not silently leak into services that did not subscribe. nil (omitted) = legacy behavior: mount all keys (preserved for pre-v0.16.10 services on upgrade). Empty list = inherit nothing. Use `kuso env share/unshare` or the dashboard chip toggle to edit.

sleep.description:
Cold-pause behaviour. When enabled, the operator scales
the deployment to 0 after `afterMinutes` of no inbound
traffic; the first request wakes it back up.

DO NOT enable for services that receive third-party
webhooks or payment callbacks (Stripe, ePay, GitHub,
Slack, etc.). Cold-start can exceed the sender's
retry timeout, producing duplicate or late
deliveries. Safe for backoffice tools, internal
dashboards, and preview environments.

For services that mix callbacks with other traffic,
set wakeOn.excludePaths so the deployment stays
warm even when sleep.enabled is true. Note: the
semantic is "if any path matters, the whole
deployment stays warm" — kube can't route per-path
inside one Deployment.

wakeOn.description:
Must-stay-warm signals. When excludePaths is
non-empty, the operator keeps the deployment
at min 1 even when scale.Min=0 would otherwise
idle it. Tickero's payment-callback use case:
wakeOn.excludePaths: ["/api/v1/payments/notify"]
means /api/v1/payments/notify is reachable 24/7
and the rest of the service stays warm too
(per-path isolation needs a service split).

subscribedAddons.description: Per-service opt-in list of project addon short names whose <addon>-conn secret gets mounted on this service's pods. nil = legacy auto-mount-all (pre-v0.16.23); non-nil = explicit subscription. Same opt-in model as sharedEnvKeys, but for addon conn-secrets (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, NATS_URL etc).

volumes.description: Persistent volumes to mount into every env's pod. Each entry maps to a PVC + a volumeMount. Declared explicitly so the apiserver doesn't prune them on write.
